name: GTM Marketing Program Lifecycle
description: A reviewed marketing-program plan with narrowly approved HubSpot changes, consent decisions isolated behind their own gate, and provider-safe execution evidence.
audience: Marketing operations teams managing HubSpot campaign programs
problem: Marketing program setup becomes unsafe when audience membership, campaign metadata, draft assets, consent state, and delivery are changed as one opaque operation.
outcome: A reviewed marketing-program plan with narrowly approved HubSpot changes, consent decisions isolated behind their own gate, and provider-safe execution evidence.
whyAi: AI can reconcile program context and prepare bounded changes while StackOS keeps provider execution, approvals, idempotency, and evidence explicit.
operatorPath: Choose one program scope, audience boundary, and review-only or apply mode.
Review campaign, draft-email, segment, and consent changes separately.
Approve ordinary program mutations and legal consent changes through distinct gates.
agentPath: Inspect only the HubSpot state needed for the bounded program.
Prepare safe-ref changes and isolate conflicts, missing consent, and unavailable capabilities.
Execute only approved actions with stable mutation keys and reconcile provider-safe evidence.
recovery: If a safe ref, scope, or consent fact is missing, keep the program in review-only state.
After a partial provider result, inspect the existing action-call evidence and retry only unresolved mutations with the same keys.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop after the reviewed plan when writes are not authorized.
Stop before consent mutation when legal basis or affirmative consent evidence is incomplete.
prerequisites: A HubSpot OAuth connection with the Marketing capability bundle
A bounded program scope and safe campaign, email, segment, contact, and subscription refs where applicable
Audience policy, legal-basis policy, and separate approval authority for consent changes
proof: Reviewed program and consent change sets
Action-call refs, provider request ids, partial failures, and retry guidance
whenToUse: A HubSpot marketing program needs coordinated audience, campaign, draft-email, or consent review and approved updates.

stages.title: Bound Marketing Program
stages.summary: Read the goal, bounded scope, execution mode, durable context, and prior learnings.

stages.title: Inspect Current Program State
stages.summary: Read only the campaigns, draft emails, segments, subscription definitions, and selected contact preferences needed by the plan.

stages.title: Prepare Reviewed Change Sets
stages.summary: Produce atomic program and consent change sets, expected effects, idempotency inputs, and explicit skips.

stages.title: Apply Approved Program Changes
stages.summary: Optionally create or update campaign and draft-email assets and change bounded segment membership.

stages.title: Apply Separately Approved Consent Changes
stages.summary: Optionally apply explicit contact preference changes after independent legal and evidence review.

stages.title: Reconcile Program Evidence
stages.summary: Store the durable program index and final status without claiming publication, bulk delivery, or unobserved outcomes.
